name: Oxygen therapy
description: Supplemental oxygen therapy and its delivery route (nasal cannula, mask, Venturi, non-rebreather, high-flow, etc.); not flow rate or FiO2.

en.name: Oxygen therapy
en.shortDescription: Supplemental oxygen therapy and its delivery route (nasal cannula, mask, Venturi, non-rebreather, high-flow, etc.); not flow rate or FiO2.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Oxygen therapy is the delivery of supplemental oxygen to a patient to raise the oxygen content of the gas they breathe. In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating that oxygen was administered, usually capturing the delivery interface (nasal cannula, face mask, Venturi mask, non-rebreather mask, high-flow nasal cannula, etc.). There is no unit. It is recorded across intensive care, emergency, anaesthesia and ward settings. The set covers the act and route of oxygen delivery — it is distinct from the oxygen *flow rate* (L/min) and the *fraction of inspired oxygen* (FiO2), which are numeric measurements in their own concept sets. It is scoped to acute, in-hospital oxygen administration (chronic/home oxygen-therapy contexts are excluded — see Excluded Concepts).

## Included Concepts

The set is anchored on the SNOMED procedure *Oxygen therapy* with its descendants, restricted to acute in-hospital oxygen delivery. Grouped here by what they describe.

### Generic oxygen therapy (default)

- *4239130 / 57485005 — Oxygen therapy (SNOMED)*: the general concept; the default when the source records oxygen administration without a specific route.
- *4074661 / 243136002 — Short-term oxygen therapy (SNOMED)*: acute, time-limited oxygen therapy.

### By delivery interface / route

- *4155151 / 371907003 — Oxygen administration by nasal cannula (SNOMED)*.
- *4216695 / 71786000 — Intranasal oxygen therapy (SNOMED)*.
- *4162736 / 371908008 — Oxygen administration by mask (SNOMED)*.
- *4323763 / 429253002 — Oxygen administration by Venturi mask (SNOMED)*: fixed-concentration (air-entrainment) mask.
- *44790731 / 240051000000102 — Oxygen administration by non rebreather mask (SNOMED)*: high-concentration reservoir mask.
- *44790798 / 243011000000108 — Oxygen administration by mask with bag and valve (SNOMED)*.
- *44806745 / 802801000000109 — Oxygen administration by mask at 60 percent concentration (SNOMED)*.
- *37155203 / 149761000000108 — Administration of oxygen using tracheostomy mask (SNOMED)*.

### High-flow / humidified

- *3655950 / 870533002 — Heated and humidified high flow oxygen therapy (SNOMED)* and *37158406 / 1259025002 — Heated and humidified high flow oxygen therapy using nasal cannula (SNOMED)*: high-flow nasal oxygen (HFNC/HFNO).
- *4119964 / 304577004 — Humidified oxygen therapy (SNOMED)*.

### By concentration / flow level

- *4201025 / 315041000 — High concentration oxygen therapy (SNOMED)*.
- *1074232 / 1336169000 — Low flow oxygen delivery (SNOMED)*.

## Excluded Concepts

Concepts pulled in by the SNOMED *Oxygen therapy* hierarchy that are out of scope for acute intensive-care oxygen administration are excluded:

- **Oxygen-delivery optimisation / oxygen dynamics** — *4189560 / 391893003 — Manipulation of oxygen dynamics* and *4136465 / 398077001 — Oxygen dynamic interventions* with their descendants (haemodynamic/metabolic interventions, not oxygen administration).
- **Oxygenator therapy** — *4082249 / 182714002 — Oxygenator therapy (SNOMED)*: an extracorporeal membrane oxygenator (ECMO), not interface-delivered oxygen.
- **Chronic / home-care contexts** — *4072513 / 243137006 — Long-term oxygen therapy*, *4140455 / 426990007 — Home oxygen therapy*, *44788930 / 201661000000106 — Ambulatory oxygen therapy*, *44788929 / 201651000000108 — SBOT - Short-burst oxygen therapy* (all SNOMED): outpatient/home oxygen contexts, not an acute in-hospital delivery.
- **Niche / non-ICU methods** — *4056814 / 182713008 — Catalytic oxygen therapy*, *37312322 / 788538002 — Alcohol-oxygen vapor therapy*, *603260 / 1363901000000105 — Oxygen administration using headbox* (neonatal hood), and *4173351 / 276732005 — Apneic oxygenation* (a peri-intubation technique rather than a continuous oxygen-delivery mode) (all SNOMED).

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records that oxygen was given without specifying the interface: *4239130 / 57485005 — Oxygen therapy*. Map to a specific interface concept only when the source documents it.
- **Therapy/route vs measurements**: this set is the *act and route* of oxygen delivery. The oxygen *flow rate* (L/min) belongs to the Oxygen flow rate concept set, and the inspired oxygen *fraction* (FiO2, %) to the Fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) concept set — do not map a numeric value here.
- **High-flow nasal oxygen**: map HFNC/HFNO to *37158406 / 1259025002 — Heated and humidified high flow oxygen therapy using nasal cannula*. The high-flow nasal cannula *device* itself is not part of this therapy set.
- **Out of scope**: chronic/home oxygen therapy, apneic oxygenation, ECMO/membrane oxygenation and oxygen-delivery optimisation are not mapped here.

fr.name: Oxygénothérapie
fr.shortDescription: Oxygénothérapie et sa voie d’administration (lunettes, masque, Venturi, non-rebreather, haut-débit, etc.) ; ni débit ni FiO2.

createdByDetails.affiliation: Univ Rennes, CHU Rennes, INSERM, LTSI-UMR 1099, Rennes, France
createdByDetails.profession: Medical Informatics and Intensive Care Physician

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Rename Oxygen device -> Oxygen therapy. Re-anchor on Oxygen therapy (4239130) with descendants; exclude the oxygen-delivery-optimisation branch (Manipulation of oxygen dynamics 4189560, Oxygen dynamic interventions 4136465) and Oxygenator therapy/ECMO (4082249). Dropped the HFNC device (4139525) to keep the set homogeneous (procedures). Resolves to 23. Added clinical description.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Exclude non-ICU concepts pulled in by the Oxygen therapy anchor: catalytic oxygen therapy (4056814), alcohol-oxygen vapor therapy (37312322), headbox (603260, neonatal), and the chronic/home-care contexts long-term (4072513), home (4140455), ambulatory (44788930) and short-burst (44788929) oxygen therapy, and apneic oxygenation (4173351, a peri-intubation technique).
